hero.title: Home Mortgage Calculator
hero.subtitle: Plan your home purchase safely by calculating exactly how much you'll pay each month with comparative Price vs SAC tables.

formula_explanation: This standard amortization formula calculates the fixed monthly payment required to fully pay off a loan of a specific amount, interest rate, and term.
example: A $250,000 home loan at 5.5% interest for 30 years will cost you $1,419.47 per month, resulting in $261,010 in total interest paid over the life of the loan.

faq.question: What is loan amortization?
faq.answer: Amortization is the process of spreading out a loan into a series of fixed payments. While the payment stays the same, the portion going towards interest vs. principal changes over time.

faq.question: How can I lower my interest?
faq.answer: You can lower your total interest paid by securing a lower rate, choosing a shorter term, or making extra principal payments each month.

faq.question: Should I make extra principal payments?
faq.answer: Yes. Extra payments apply directly to your principal balance, reducing the amount of money that accrues interest. This shortens your loan term and saves you significant money.

faq.question: What is the difference between fixed and variable rates?
faq.answer: A fixed-rate keeps the exact same interest rate for the entire lifespan. A variable rate fluctuates based on market indexes, meaning your monthly payments can change.
